title: mentha × alopecuroides var. lamarckii
intro: Mentha × alopecuroides var. lamarckii, also known as Lemon Mint, is a hybrid plant species in the mint family, derived from the cross between Mentha × piperita (Peppermint) and Mentha suaveolens (Apple Mint). This plant is valued for its medicinal properties, including its ability to alleviate digestive issues, reduce fever, and relieve headaches, due to its high concentration of menthol and menthone. The health benefits of Lemon Mint include its anti-inflammatory properties, which may help to soothe sore throats and ease respiratory issues, as well as its potential to improve digestion and reduce nausea. Lemon Mint is often used in herbal preparations such as teas, tinctures, and infusions, which can be consumed orally to reap its medicinal benefits.
